content:
I am a software and machine-learning engineer and researcher at Iowa State University, pursuing a B.S. in Data Science and an M.S. in Mechanical Engineering, and conducting research at the Translational AI Center (TrAC) with Professor Soumik Sarkar.
My M.S. thesis develops agentic vision-language systems for cyber-agriculture. I co-first-authored SAGE, an autonomous, symptom-grounded crop-disease-diagnosis agent built on the largest plant-disease image–symptom dataset to date, accepted at a CVPR 2026 workshop. More broadly, I build end-to-end systems — from web applications and data pipelines to large-scale LLM/VLM evaluation and reproducible benchmarks — spanning agriculture, healthcare, and finance, and I care about turning research prototypes into reliable, well-tested, production-minded software.
summary: I am a software and machine-learning engineer and researcher at Iowa State University, pursuing a B.S. in Data Science and an M.S. in Mechanical Engineering, and conducting research at the Translational AI Center (TrAC) with Professor Soumik Sarkar.
title: Tirtho Roy

content: Highlights Built a walk-forward backtesting framework benchmarking classical, neural, and world-model predictors on a global equity dataset. Isolated the conditions under which engineered features outperform raw price inputs across model families.
summary: Walk-forward benchmark isolating when engineered features beat raw prices across classical, neural, and world-model predictors.
title: When Do Engineered Features Beat Raw Prices? A Walk-Forward Benchmark of Classical, Neural, and World-Model Predictors on a Global Equity Dataset

content: Highlights Co-built the largest plant-disease image–symptom dataset to date: 335 crops, 1,251 disease classes, ~839K images with source-grounded, expert-validated symptom descriptions. Engineered stepwise diagnostic reasoning (anatomical context → symptom-guided narrowing → reference-image comparison → explainable trace). Symptom grounding improved accuracy by +16.2 points on average and extends to new crops with no retraining. Core component of M.S. thesis research (co-first author).
summary: Autonomous, symptom-grounded VLM agent for crop-disease diagnosis on the largest plant-disease image–symptom dataset to date.
title: SAGE: Scalable Agentic Grounded Evaluation for Crop Disease Diagnosis

content: Highlights Built the first unified benchmark for pathway-guided cancer therapy-response modeling (BINN, GraphPath, PATH) under identical conditions. Multi-task pipeline over 2,622 TCGA patients across 5 cohorts using Reactome pathway-activity features. Best model reached 0.92 AUROC on prostate targeted-therapy prediction at 11% class prevalence.
summary: First unified benchmark for pathway-guided cancer therapy-response modeling across BINN, GraphPath, and PATH.
title: TRAPS: Therapeutic Response Analysis via Pathway-informed Stratification

content: Highlights Built a multi-agent iterative-refinement loop: generators DeepSeek R1 + Med-PaLM, evaluators LLaMA 3.1 + Phi-4. Aligned outputs to AMA Principles of Medical Ethics and a five-tier Safety Risk Assessment (SRA-5). Cut ethical violations by 89% and achieved a 92% risk-downgrade rate over 900 clinical queries across nine ethical domains.
summary: Multi-agent generator/evaluator loops aligning medical LLM outputs to AMA ethics and a five-tier safety assessment.
title: Improving the Safety and Trustworthiness of Medical AI via Multi-Agent Evaluation Loops

content: Highlights Co-developed a reinforcement-learning intervention engine for syndemic health management. Modeled intervention timing and selection as a sequential decision problem.
summary: Reinforcement-learning intervention engine modeling syndemic health management as sequential decision-making.
title: Intelligent Health Intervention System for Syndemic Management Among University Students: An AI-Driven Approach Using Reinforcement Learning

content: Highlights First-authored an analysis interpreting transformer architectures through physics and dynamical-systems theory to explain their internal computation dynamics.
summary: Interpreting transformer internal computation dynamics via physics and dynamical-systems theory (first author).
title: Interpreting Transformers through the Lens of Physics and Dynamical Systems

content: Highlights Designed an agentic, pathway-grounded tree-of-thought inference pipeline structuring LLM reasoning over biological pathway knowledge for oncology therapeutic reasoning.
summary: Pathway-grounded tree-of-thought agent for mechanistic therapeutic reasoning in oncology.
title: An Agentic Framework for Mechanistic Therapeutic Reasoning in Oncology via Pathway-Grounded Tree-of-Thought Inference

content: Highlights Benchmarked LLaMA on multi-class healthcare test-result prediction. Assessed reliability for responsible medical-AI deployment.
summary: Benchmarking LLaMA on multi-class healthcare test-result prediction for responsible medical-AI deployment.
title: LlamaHealth: Assessing LLaMA's Performance in Multi-Class Healthcare Test Result Prediction

content: Highlights Established baseline benchmarks for Bioemu-1 on protein structural-ensemble prediction. Defined reproducible metrics for computational structural biology.
summary: Baseline benchmarks and reproducible metrics for protein structural-ensemble prediction.
title: Benchmarking Bioemu-1: Establishing a Baseline for Protein Structural Ensemble Prediction

content: Highlights Built an LLM-powered tool to estimate and analyze carbon footprint from operational data.
summary: LLM-powered tool to estimate and analyze carbon footprint from operational data.
title: Emissai: LLM-Powered Analysis of Carbon Footprint

content: Highlights Designed a privacy-preserving federated-learning framework for multi-modal 2D/3D dental imaging. Featured cross-modal prompt alignment, hierarchical multi-modal optimization, and Byzantine-resilient aggregation with differential privacy.
summary: Privacy-preserving federated learning for multi-modal 2D/3D dental imaging with differential privacy.
title: Federated In-Context Prompt Selection for Multi-Modal 3D Dental Imaging: A Theoretical Framework with Privacy-Preserving Guarantees

content: Highlights Prototyped the multi-agent medical-LLM safety framework (generators DeepSeek R1 / Med-PaLM; evaluators LLaMA 3.1 / Phi-4). Aligned to AMA principles and the SRA-5 protocol; later extended into the arXiv study “Improving the Safety and Trustworthiness of Medical AI via Multi-Agent Evaluation Loops”.
summary: Poster prototype of the multi-agent medical-LLM safety framework (later extended to a full study).
title: Enhancing Medical AI Safety Through Multi-Agent Evaluation and Iterative Refinement

content: Highlights Co-developed AgEval, a 12-task plant-stress phenotyping benchmark for VLMs (Claude, GPT, Gemini, LLaVA). Best-model F1 improved 46.24% → 73.37% (8-shot); strategic example selection added +15.38% F1. Introduced a coefficient-of-variation metric revealing per-class gaps of 26–58%.
summary: AgEval — a 12-task plant-stress phenotyping benchmark for zero-/few-shot in-context learning of VLMs.
title: Leveraging Vision Language Models for Specialized Agricultural Tasks

content: Highlights Built a GPT-4o analysis pipeline over 430 Shark Tank pitches. Combined expert annotation with statistical validation to calibrate LLM-derived predictions for a peer-reviewed B2B marketing study.
summary: GPT-4o analysis of 430 Shark Tank pitches quantifying how market orientation and brand storytelling drive investor decisions.
title: The Impact of Market Orientation and Brand Storytelling on Shark Tank Evaluations

content:
Overview Collaborated with Professor Ryan McGhee at the ISU Department of Agricultural and Biosystems Engineering to develop a versatile chatbot frontend that significantly improves user experience and interface design.
Key Responsibilities Frontend development and architecture User interface design and enhancement User experience optimization Integration with backend systems Iterative design based on user feedback Skills Applied Modern web frameworks and technologies Responsive UI design User-centered design principles Software development best practices
summary: Developed a versatile chatbot frontend to improve user experience and interface design for agricultural and biosystems engineering applications.
title: Chatbot Frontend Development & UI Enhancement

content:
Overview Developed novel approaches to enhance the safety and ethical compliance of medical LLM outputs. This research addresses critical challenges in deploying AI for healthcare:
Multi-agent evaluation frameworks using specialized LLMs as evaluator agents Iterative refinement against established healthcare standards (AMA-9 Principles) Safety Risk Assessment (SRA-5) tier implementation Byzantine-resilient aggregation with differential privacy guarantees Key Achievements ACM-BCB 2025 Presentation - Poster presentation at The 16th ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, and Health Informatics Developed framework pairing generative models (DeepSeek R1, Med-PaLM) with evaluator agents (LLaMA 3.1, Phi-4) Focus on real-world deployment challenges and regulatory compliance
summary: Developing multi-agent frameworks to ensure medical AI systems are safe, ethical, and compliant with healthcare standards and regulations.
title: Medical AI Safety & Ethical Alignment

content:
Overview Exploring how state-of-the-art vision-language models (VLMs) can be adapted and optimized for specialized agricultural tasks. This research contributes to advancing precision agriculture through AI.
Research Focus Benchmarking VLMs on agricultural datasets Specialized prompt engineering for farm-related tasks Cross-modal understanding of agricultural imagery Real-world deployment in agricultural systems Publications WACV 2025 - “Leveraging Vision Language Models for Specialized Agricultural Tasks” (IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision) Research on protein structural prediction (Bioemu-1 benchmarking at AIChE 2025) Applications Crop disease and pest identification Yield estimation Field condition assessment Climate-aware agricultural decision support
summary: Leveraging vision-language models and computer vision to address specialized challenges in agricultural applications, including crop monitoring, disease detection, and yield prediction.
title: Vision-Language Models for Agriculture

content:
Overview Collaborating with Professor Soumik Sarkar at the Translational AI Center to explore how LLMs can enhance decision-making processes in finance. This research includes:
Analyzing how LLMs interpret market signals and business fundamentals Studying investor behavior through the lens of AI analysis (Shark Tank data) Developing frameworks for AI-assisted financial analysis Understanding market orientation and brand storytelling effects on investment decisions Key Contributions Published research on market orientation and startup pitch evaluation (JBIM, REU 2025) Developed LLM-based analytical frameworks for entrepreneurship and investment analysis Presented findings at TrAC Day 2025 and REU Summer Symposium 2025
summary: Leveraging Large Language Models to improve financial decision-making, with applications to startup evaluation and investor behavior analysis.
title: LLMs in Finance: Enhancing Decision-Making Processes
